vBRIEFInfo.description: Freeze-prep story E decomposed from GitHub issue #1912 (pinned legacy->bridge->npm e2e leg)

plan.title: feat(#1912 E): pinned legacy->bridge->npm-hybrid e2e leg (reads Tier-0 SoT, pending-pin until frozen)

narratives.Description: Freeze-prep story E of the #1912 capstone. Adds the Tier-1 pinned migration e2e leg: legacy on-disk layout (pre-v0.27 / git-clone / submodule) -> the frozen Go bridge installer (stage 1) -> npm hybrid via `directive migrate` + `directive init`/`update` (stage 2). Mirrors the existing `task release:e2e` harness shape (packages/core/src/release-e2e/). The leg reads the Tier-0 SoT (`lastGoInstaller()` from packages/core/src/legacy-bridge/sot.ts, landed in story G #1969) for which bridge tag to pin. SEMANTICS: pending-pin -- while the SoT is null (unfrozen, today), the leg cannot download/run a real frozen bridge binary, so it runs the fixture-shape assertions it can and SKIPS the real frozen-binary download with a clear advisory ('pending SoT pin'); once the operator freezes (SoT set), the same leg runs the real pinned bridge->npm handoff. This satisfies the #1912 'pinned migration e2e' Tier-1 checkbox and the 'freeze-on-real-handoff' sequencing intent without itself cutting the freeze.
narratives.Origin: Decomposed from https://github.com/deftai/directive/issues/1912 (freeze-prep). Depends on story G (the SoT module) which is now merged on master.
narratives.Overview:
## What to build

A new e2e leg in the release-e2e harness exercising the two-stage bridge:
1. Provision a LEGACY-layout fixture in a throwaway dir (at least: pre-v0.27 sentinel-only AGENTS.md / git-clone-style / orphan `.deft/VERSION`).
2. Stage 1: run the frozen Go bridge installer to normalize -> canonical-vendored `.deft/core` + correct AGENTS.md managed-section + handshake VERSION. (While SoT null: the locally-built Go installer may stand in for shape assertions, but the REAL frozen-tag download is skipped as pending-pin.)
3. Stage 2: run `directive migrate` (npm-managed stamp) then `directive init`/`update`, asserting the end state is a valid npm-hybrid deposit.
4. Read the pin from `lastGoInstaller()`; emit `PENDING (SoT unfrozen)` vs run-for-real when frozen. Three-state-friendly result consistent with the other e2e legs.

Expose via a `--legacy-bridge` flag on the release-e2e CLI (parseE2EFlags / ParsedE2EFlags / runE2e + cmdReleaseE2e in main.ts), defaulting OFF so it does not slow the default `task release:e2e` budget (mirror the `--skip-npm` pattern). Soft-skip cleanly when the Go toolchain / npm is absent.

## Must NOT
- Cut/tag/build/freeze any Go installer release, or set the SoT value (operator-only).
- Hardcode a bridge version -- read `lastGoInstaller()`; carry the `deft:last-go-installer` sentinel marker on any line that STATES the version so the drift gate stays green.
- Touch the SoT module, the freeze/drift gates (story G), or the npm legacy refuse-preflight surfaces (story P, merged).

## Integration points
- Mirror the npm-ops leg (`packages/core/src/release-e2e/npm-ops.ts`) structure: a self-contained function returning [ok, reason], invoked from `main.ts` behind the new flag.
- Reuse `release-e2e/git-ops.ts` for the throwaway-repo provisioning + cleanup try/finally pattern.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
- packages/core/src/release-e2e/legacy-bridge-leg.ts (+ .test.ts): the pinned legacy->bridge->npm leg; reads lastGoInstaller(); pending-pin advisory when null.
- packages/core/src/release-e2e/flags.ts + types.ts: add `--legacy-bridge` flag (default off).
- packages/core/src/release-e2e/main.ts: invoke the leg behind the flag; thread the result into the summary.
- Tests: fixture-shape assertions for each legacy input; pending-pin path (SoT null) emits PENDING and exits ok; a frozen-SoT path (monkeypatched/seam) runs the real handoff assertions.
narratives.UserStory: As a deft maintainer preparing the freeze, I want a pinned legacy->bridge->npm e2e leg that reads the SoT and stays pending until I freeze, so that the moment I pin the bridge tag the two-stage migration is regression-guarded end-to-end before I rely on it.

narrative.Acceptance: When the --legacy-bridge leg runs, it provisions a legacy-layout fixture, drives it through the bridge to canonical-vendored, then through directive migrate/init to a valid npm-hybrid deposit.

items.title: leg reads the Tier-0 SoT and is pending-pin while null

narrative.Acceptance: When lastGoInstaller() is null, the leg emits a PENDING (SoT unfrozen) advisory and does not attempt a real frozen-binary download; when set, it runs the real pinned handoff.

items.title: leg is opt-in and does not slow default release:e2e

narrative.Acceptance: When task release:e2e runs without --legacy-bridge, the new leg does not execute; with the flag it runs and reports a [ok, reason] result.

items.title: no freeze, no SoT edit, no hardcoded version

narrative.Acceptance: When the story lands, the SoT value stays null, no Go release is cut, and any version reference reads the SoT (drift gate stays green).

references.title: Issue #1912: build + freeze the last Go installer (parent capstone)

expected_outputs: opt-in --legacy-bridge e2e leg drives legacy->bridge->npm-hybrid; reads SoT; pending-pin advisory while null; drift gate stays green
swarm.missing_traces_justification: Traced to #1912 Tier-1 'pinned migration e2e' checkbox + freeze-on-real-handoff sequencing; depends on the now-merged story G SoT module.
